La secciรณn de Eventos es tu espacio par: The Events section is your space to create and self-manage Brawl Stars competitions: tournaments and leagues that you can open to the platform's whole community or keep private to enjoy only with your club, your group or your friends. The idea is that BrawlSensei doesn't stop at individual analytics, but also serves as a meeting point to organize activity around the game and bring life to the community behind it.

Montar uno lleva un momento: eliges si e: Setting one up takes a moment: you choose whether it's a league or tournament, individual or team-based, its format (Swiss, single elimination, round robinโฆ) and the rules (rounds, points, modes and maps). You sign up players by their ID (or let them register themselves from the public board), generate each round and record the results; the system can even detect them on its own from your friendly matches. The standings update automatically and followers receive notifications about what interests them, so all that's left for you is to enjoy the competition.

Sistema McMahon: como el suizo, pero con: McMahon system: like Swiss, but with starting points based on each player's level or category. Ideal for many participants of varying levels.
Sistema suizo: cada ronda empareja a jug: Swiss system: each round pairs players with a similar score. There is no elimination; a fixed number of rounds is played and whoever scores the most points wins.

Solo lo que sale de tus registros de bat: Only what comes from your battle logs: wins, matches played, win rate (over a minimum number of matches), win streaks, distinct brawlers you win with, net trophies and times you're Star Player. Each goal can be narrowed to a brawler or a mode. No manual data (not "deal X damage"): if the app doesn't record it, it can't be measured.
Solo puedes inscribir cada ID una vez, p: You can only register each ID once, but you can sign up several IDs from your account.
